author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXVI. 17, 18.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now the first day of the feast of unleavened bread the disciples came to Jesus, saying, Where wilt Thou that we prepare for Thee to eat the Passover?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said, Go into the city to such a man, and say unto him, The Master saith, My time is at hand; I will keep the Passover at thy house with My disciples.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By the first day of the feast of unleavened bread, he means the day before that feast; for they are accustomed always to reckon the day from the evening, and he makes mention of this in which in the evening the passover must be killed;for on the fifth day of the week they came unto Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this onecalls the day before the feast of unleavened bread,speaking of the time when they came to Him, and another saith on this wise, “Then came the day of unleavened bread, when the passover must be killed;”by the word “came,” meaning this, it was nigh, it was at the doors, making mention plainly of that evening.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they began with the evening, wherefore also each adds, when the passover was killed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they say, “Where wilt Thou that we prepare for Thee to eat the passover?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So even from this it is manifest, that He had no house, no place of sojourning; and I suppose neither had they.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For surely they would have entreated him to come there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But neither had they any, having now parted with all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore did He keep the passover?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To indicate by all things unto the last day, that He is not opposed to the law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for what possible reason doth He send them to an unknown person?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To show by this also that He might have avoided suffering.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He who prevailed over this man’s mind, so that he received them, and that by words; what would He not have done with them that crucified Him, if it had been His will not to suffer?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what He did about the ass, this He did here also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there too He saith, “If any man say aught unto you, ye shall say, that the Lord hath need of them;”and so likewise here, “The Master saith, I will keep the passover at thy house.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But I marvel not at this only, that he received Him, being unknown, but that expecting to bring upon himself such enmity and implacable hostility, he despised the enmity of the multitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, because they knew him not, He gave them a sign, like as the prophet touching Saul, saying, “Thou shalt find one going up and carrying a bottle;”and here, “carrying a pitcher.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see again the display of his power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He did not only say, “I will keep the passover,” but He adds another thing also, “My time is at hand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He did, at once continually reminding His disciples of the passion, so that exercised by the frequency of the prediction, they should be prepared for what was to take place; and at the same time to show to themselves, and to him that was receiving Him, and to all the Jews, which I have often mentioned, that not involuntarily doth He come to His passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He adds, “with my disciples,” in order that both the preparation should be sufficient, and that the man should not suppose that He was concealing Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now when the even was come, He sat down with the twelve disciples.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Oh the shamelessness of Judas!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he too was present there, and came to partake both of the mysteries, and of the meal,and is convicted at the very table, when although he had been a wild beast, he would have become tame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause the evangelist also signifies, that while they are eating, Christ speaks of His betrayal, that both by the time and by the table he might show the wickedness of the traitor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when the disciples had done, as Jesus had appointed them, “when the even was come, He sat down with the twelve.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as they did eat, He said,” we are told, “Verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And before the supper, He had even washed his feet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how He spares the traitor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not, such a one shall betray me; but, “one of you,” so as again to give him power of repentance by concealment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He chooseth to alarm all, for the sake of saving this man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of you, the twelve, saith He, that are everywhere present with me, whose feet I washed, to whom I promised so many things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Intolerable sorrow thereupon seized that holy company.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And John indeed saith, they “were in doubt, and looked one upon another,”and each of them asked in fear concerning himself, although conscious to themselves of no such thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this evangelist saith, that “being exceeding sorrowful, they began every one of them to say unto Him, Is it I, Lord?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He answered and said, He it is, to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Mark at what time He discovered him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was when it was His will to deliver the rest from this trouble, for they were even dead with the fear, wherefore also they were instant with their questions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not only as desiring to deliver them from their distress He did this, but also as willing to amend the traitor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since after having often heard it generally, he continued incorrigible, being past feeling, He being minded to make him feel more, takes off his mask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when being sorrowful they began to say, “Is it I, Lord?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He answered and said, He that dippethwith me in the dish, the same shall betray me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The Son of Man goeth, as it is written of Him, but woe to the man bywhom the Son of Man is betrayed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It had been good for that man if he had not been born.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now some say that he was so bold as not to honor his Master, but to dip with Him: but to me Christ seems to have done this too, to shame him the more, and bring him over to a better disposition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this act again has something more in it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things we ought not to pass by at random, but they should be infixed in our minds, and wrath would find no place at any time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who, bearing in mind that supper, and the traitor sitting at meat with the Saviour of all, and Him who was to be betrayed thus meekly reasoning, would not put away all venom of wrath and anger?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See at any rate how meekly He conducts Himself towards him, “The Son of Man goeth, as it is written of Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things again He said, both to restore the disciples, that they might not think the thing was a sign of weakness, and to amend the traitor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But woe unto thatman by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! it had been good for that man if he had not been born.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See again in His rebukes His unspeakable meekness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not even here with invective, but more in the way of compassion, doth He apply what He saith, but in a disguised way again; and yet not his former senselessness only, but his subsequent shamelessness was deserving of the utmost indignation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after this conviction he saith, “Is it I, Lord?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Oh insensibility!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He inquires, when conscious to himself of such things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the evangelist too, marvelling at his boldness, saith this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith the most mild and gentle Jesus?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Thou sayest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet He might have said, O thou unholy, thou all unholy one; accursed, and profane; so long a time in travail with mischief, who hast gone thy way, and made satanical compacts, and hast agreed to receive money, and hast been convicted by me too, dost thou yet dare to ask?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But none of these things did He say; but how?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Thou sayest?” fixing for us bounds and rules of long suffering.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But some one will say, Yet if it was written that He was to suffer these things, wherefore is Judas blamed, for he did the things that were written?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not with this intent, but from wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou inquire not concerning the motive, thou wilt deliver even the devil from the charges against him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things are not, they are not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For both the one and the other are deserving of countless punishments, although the world was saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither did the treason of Judas work out salvation for us, but the wisdom of Christ, and the good contrivance of His fair skill, using the wickednesses of others for our advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “What then,” one may say, “though Judas had not betrayed Him, would not another have betrayed Him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what has this to do with the question?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Because if Christ must needs be crucified, it must be by the means of some one, and if by some one, surely by such a person as this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if all had been good, the dispensation in our behalf had been impeded.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the Allwise knows how He shall bring about our benefits, even had this happened.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For His wisdom is rich in contrivance, and incomprehensible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So for this reason, that no one might suppose that Judas had become a minister of the dispensation, He declares the wretchedness of the man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But some one will say again, “And if it had been good if he had never been born, wherefore did He suffer both this man, and all the wicked, to come into the world?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When thou oughtest to blame the wicked, for that having the power not to become such as they are, they have become wicked, thou leavest this, and busiest thyself, and art curious about the things of God; although knowing that it is not by necessity that any one is wicked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But the good only should be born,” he would say, “and there were no need of hell, nor punishment, nor vengeance, nor trace of vice, but the wicked should either not be born at all, or being born should straightway depart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: First then, it were well to repeat to thee the saying of the apostle, “Nay but, O man, who art thou that repliest against God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shall the thing formed say to Him that formed it, Why hast Thou made me thus?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou still demandest reasons, we would say this, that the good are more admired for being among the bad; because their long-suffering and great self-command is then most shown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou takest away the occasion of their wrestlings, and conflicts, by saying these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “What then, in order that these may appear good, are others punished?” saith he.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: God forbid, but for their own wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither because they were brought into the world did they become wicked, but on account of their own wickedness; wherefore also they are punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For how should they fail to be deserving of punishment, seeing they have so many teachers of virtue, and gain nothing therefrom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as the noble and good are worthy of double honor, because they both became good, and took no hurt from the wicked; so also the worthless deserve twofold punishment, both because they became wicked, when they might have become good (they show it who have become such), and because they gained nothing from the good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us see what saith this wretched man, when convicted by his Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Is it I, Rabbi?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why did he not ask this from the beginning?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He thought to escape knowledge by its being said, “one of you;” but when He had made him manifest, he ventured again to ask, confiding in the clemency of his Master, that He would not convict him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: O blindness!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whereunto hath it led him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such is covetousness, it renders men fools and senseless, yea reckless, and dogs instead of men, or rather even more fierce than dogs, and devils after being dogs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This man at least received unto him the devil even when plotting against him, but Jesus, even when doing him good, he betrayed, having already become a devil in will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such doth the insatiable desire of gain make men, out of their mind, frenzy-smitten, altogether given up to gain, as was the case even with Judas.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But how do Matthew and the other evangelists say, that, when he made the agreement touching the treason, then the devil seized him; but John, that “after the sop Satan entered into him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And John himself knew this, for further back he saith, “The devil having now put into the heart of Judas, that he should betray Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then doth he say, “After the sop Satan entered into him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because he enters not in suddenly, nor at once, but makes much trial first, which accordingly was done here also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after having tried him in the beginning, and assailed him quietly, after that he saw him prepared to receive him, he thenceforth wholly breathed himself into him, and completely got the better of him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But how, if they were eating the passover, did they eat it contrary to the law?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they should not have eaten it, sitting down to their meat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then can be said?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That after eating it, they then sat down to the banquet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But another evangelist saith, that on that evening He not only ate the passover, but also said, “With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you,”that is, on that year.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what reason?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because then the salvation of the world was to be brought about, and the mysteries to be delivered, and the subjects of sorrow to be done away with by His death; so welcome was the cross to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But nothing softened the savage monster, nor moved, nor shamed him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He pronounced him wretched, saying, “Woe to that man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He alarmed him again, saying, “It were good for him if he had not been born.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He put him to shame, saying, “To whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And none of these things checked him, but he was seized by covetousness, as by some madness, or rather by a more grievous disease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed this is the more grievous madness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what would the madman do like this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He poured not forth foam out of his mouth but he poured forth the murder of his Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He distorted not his hands, but stretched them out for the price of precious blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore his madness was greater, because he was mad being in health.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But he doth not utter sayest thou, sounds without meaning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is more without meaning than this language.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “What will ye give me, and I will deliver Him unto you?”“I will deliver,” the devil spake by that mouth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But he did not smite the ground with his feet struggling?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, how much better so to struggle, than thus to stand upright.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But sayest thou, he did not cut himself with stones?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet how much better, than to do such things as these!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Will ye, that we bring forward the possessed and the covetous, and make a comparison between the two.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let no one account what is done a reproach to himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For we do not reproach the nature, but we lament the act.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The possessed was never clad with garments, cutting himself with stones, and running, he rushes over rough paths, driven headlong of the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do not these things seem to be dreadful?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then, if I shall show the covetous doing more grievous things than these to their own soul, and to such a degree more grievous, that these are considered child’s play compared with those.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Will you indeed shun the pest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Come then, let us see if they are in any respect in a more tolerable state than they.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In none, but even in a more grievous condition; for indeed they are more objects of shame than ten thousand naked persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it were far better to be naked as to clothing, than being clad with the fruits of covetousness, to go about like them that celebrate the orgies for Bacchus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as they have on madmen’s masks and clothes, so have these also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And much as the nakedness of the possessed is caused by madness, so doth madness produce this clothing, and the clothing is more miserable than the nakedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this I will hereby endeavor to prove.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whom should we say was more mad, amongst madmen themselves; one who should cut himself, or one who together with himself should hurt those who met him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is quite clear that it is this last.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The madmen then strip themselves of their clothing, but these all that meet them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But these tear their clothes to pieces.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how readily would every one of those that are injured consent that his garment should be torn, rather than be stripped of all his substance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But those do not aim blows at the face.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the first place, the covetous do even this, and if not all, yet do all inflict by famine and penury more grievous pains on the belly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But those bite not with the teeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Would that it were with teeth, and not with the darts of covetousness fiercer than teeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For their teeth are weapons and darts.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who will feel most pained, he that was bitten once, and straightway healed, or he that is for ever eaten up by the teeth of penury?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For penury when involuntary is more grievous than furnace or wild beast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But those rush not into the deserts like the possessed of devils.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Would it were the deserts, and not the cities, that they overran, and so all in the cities enjoyed security.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For now in this respect again, they are more intolerable than all the insane, because they do in the cities these things which the others do in the deserts, making the cities deserts, and like as in a desert, where there is none to hinder, so plundering the goods of all men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But they do not pelt with stones them that meet them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Of stones it were easy to beware; but of the wounds which by paper and ink they work to the wretched poor (framing writings full of blows without number), who, out of those that fall in with them, can ever easily beware?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And let us see also what they do to themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They walk naked up and down the city, for they have no garment of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if this doth not seem to them to be a disgrace, this again is of their exceeding madness, for that they have no feeling of the unseemliness, but while they are ashamed of having their body naked, they bear about the soul naked, and glory in it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if you wish, I will tell you also the cause of their insensibility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the cause?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They are naked amongst many that are thus naked, wherefore neither are they ashamed, like as neither are we in the baths.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that if indeed there were many clothed with virtue, then would their shame appear more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now this above all is a worthy subject for many tears, that because the bad are many, bad things are not even esteemed as a disgrace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For besides the rest, the devil hath brought about this too, not to allow them to obtain even a sense of their evil deeds, but by the multitude of them that practise wickedness, to throw a shade over their disgrace; since if it came to pass that he was in the midst of a multitude of persons practising self-restraint, such a one would see his nakedness more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That they are more naked than the possessed is evident from these things; and that they go into the deserts, neither this again could any one gainsay.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the wide and broad way is more desert than any desert.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though it have many that journey on it, yet none from amongst men, but serpents, scorpions, wolves, adders, and asps.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such are they that practise wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this way is not only desert, but much more rugged than that of the mad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is hereby evident.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For stones and ravines and crags do not so wound those that mount them, as robbery and covetousness the souls that practise them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that they live by the tombs, like the possessed, or rather that they themselves are tombs, is plain by this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is a tomb?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: A stone having a dead body lying in it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherein then do these men’s bodies differ from those stones? or rather, they are more miserable even than they.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not a stone containing a dead body, but a body more insensible than stones, bearing about a dead soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore one would not be wrong in calling them tombs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so did our Lord too call the Jews, for this reason most especially; He went on at least to say, “Their inward parts are full of ravening and covetousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Would ye that I show next, how they also cut their heads with stones?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence then first, I pray thee, wilt thou learn this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From the things here, or from the things to come?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But of the things to come they have not much regard; we must speak then of the things here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For are not anxieties more grievous than many stones, not wounding heads, but consuming a soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they are afraid, lest those things should justly go forth out of their house, which have come unto them unjustly; they tremble in fear of the utmost ills, are angry, are provoked, against those of their own house, against strangers; and now despondency, now fear, now wrath, comes upon them in succession, and they are as if they were crossing precipice after precipice, and they are earnestly looking day by day for what they have not yet acquired.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore neither do they feel pleasure in the things they have, both by reason of not feeling confidence about the security of them, and because with their whole mind they are intent upon what they have not yet seized.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And like as one continually thirsting, though he should drink up ten thousand fountains, feeleth not the pleasure, because he is not satisfied; so also these, so far from feeling pleasure, are even tormented, the more they heap around themselves; from their not feeling any limit to such desire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And things here are like this; but let us speak also of the day to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though they give not heed, yet it is necessary for us to speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In the day to come then, one will see everywhere such men as these undergoing punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when He saith, “I was an hungered, and ye gave me no meat; I was thirsty, and ye gave me no drink;” He is punishing these; and when He saith, “Depart into the eternal fire prepared for the devil,” He is sending thither them that make a bad use of riches.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the wicked servant, who gives not to his fellow-servants the goods of his Lord, is of the number of these men, and he that buried his talent, and the five virgins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whithersoever thou shalt go, thou wilt see the covetous punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And now they will hear, “There is a void between us and you;”now, “Depart from me into the fire that is prepared.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And now being cut asunder, they will go away, where there is gnashing of teeth, and from every place one may see them driven, and finding a place nowhere, but gathered in hell alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the use of the right faith to us for salvation, when we hear these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There, gnashing of teeth, and outer darkness, and the fire prepared for the devil, and to be cut asunder, and to be driven away; here, enmities, evil speakings, slanders, perils, cares, plots, to be hated of all, to be abhorred of all, even of the very persons that seem to flatter us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as good men are admired not by the good only but even by the wicked; so bad men, not the good only, but also the worthless, hate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in proof that this is true, I would gladly ask of the covetous, whether they do not feel painfully one toward another; and account such more their enemies than those that have done them the greatest wrong; whether they do not also accuse themselves, whether they do not account the thing an affront, if any one brings this reproach upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed this is an extreme reproach, and a sure proof of much wickedness; for if thou dost not endure to despise wealth, of what wilt thou ever get the better? of lust, or of the mad desire of glory, or anger, or of wrath?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how would any be persuaded of it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as to lust, and anger, and wrath, many impute it even to the temperament of the flesh, and to this do students of medicine refer the excesses thereof; and him that is of a more hot and languid temperament, they affirm to be more lustful; but him that runs out into a drier kind of ill temperament, eager, and irritable, and wrathful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But with respect to covetousness, no one ever heard of their having said any such thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So entirely is the pest the effect of mere remissness, and of a soul past feeling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, I beseech you, let us give diligence to amend all such things, and to give an opposite direction to the passions that come upon us in every age.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if in every part of our life we sail past the labors of virtue, everywhere undergoing shipwrecks; when we have arrived at the harbor destitute of spiritual freight, we shall undergo extreme punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For our present life is an outstretched ocean.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as in the sea here, there are different bays exposed to different tempests, and the Ægean is difficult because of the winds, the Tyrrhenian strait because of the confined space, the Charybdis that is by Africa because of the shallows, the Propontis, which is without the Euxine sea, on account of its violence and currents, the parts without Cadiz because of the desolation, and tracklessness, and unexplored places therein, and other portions for other causes; so also is it in our life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the first sea to view is that of our childish days, having much tempestuousness, because of its folly, its facility, because it is not steadfast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also we set over it guides and teachers, by our diligence adding what is wanting to nature, even as there by the pilot’s skill.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this age succeeds the sea of the youth, where the winds are violent as in the Ægean, lust increasing upon us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this age especially is destitute of correction; not only because he is beset more fiercely, but also because his faults are not reproved, for both teacher and guide after that withdraw.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When therefore the winds blow more fiercely, and the pilot is more feeble, and there is no helper, consider the greatness of the tempest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this there is again another period of life, that of men, in which the cares of the household press upon us, when there is a wife, and marriage, and begetting of children, and ruling of a house, and thick falling showers of cares.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then especially both covetousness flourishes and envy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When then we pass each part of our life with shipwrecks, how shall we suffice for the present life? how shall we escape future punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when first in the earliest age we learn nothing healthful, and then in youth we do not practise sobriety, and when grown to manhood do not get the better of covetousness, coming to old age as to a hold full of bilgewater, and as having made the barque of the soul weak by all these shocks, the planks being separated, we shall arrive at that harbor, bearing much filth instead of spiritual merchandise, and to the devil we shall furnish laughter, but lamentation to ourselves, and bring upon ourselves the intolerable punishments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That these things may not be, let us brace ourselves up on every side, and, withstanding all our passions, let us cast out the lust of wealth, that we may also attain unto the good things to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory forever and ever.
